aria-labels.contactMap: Map showing the location of Suliko restaurant

about.text1: At Suliko, we celebrate the authentic flavors of Georgia, combining traditional recipes with a contemporary approach to culinary craftsmanship.
about.text2: Our cuisine is a story of hospitality, shared moments around the table, and unforgettable experiences that stay with you long after the meal is over.

wine.text: Over 80 labels from Kakheti, Kartli and Racha. Orange, natural and qvevri wines - a tradition dating back nearly 8,000 years.

reservationCTA.text3: Click to continue to the full reservation form

footer.text: Authentic Georgian cuisine in the heart of Warsaw. Tradition, natural wines, and a truly memorable atmosphere.

hero.text: Have a question about our menu, reservations, or a special gathering? We’ll be happy to help and take care of every detail.

hero.text: Discover wines selected to pair with Georgian cuisine — full of aroma, depth, and the history of the regions they come from.

regionsSection.text2: Discover the flavours of our land

kakheti.name: Kakheti
kakheti.details: Kakheti is Georgia’s most important wine region, known for traditional wines aged in clay qvevri vessels. Wines from this region are deep, intense, and full of character.

kartli.name: Kartli
kartli.details: Kartli is known for elegant and well-balanced wines. They often have a fresh aroma, delicate structure, and pair beautifully with lighter dishes and appetisers.

imeretia.name: Imeretia
imeretia.details: Imeretia produces lighter, fresher, and more mineral-driven wines. Their subtle aroma and gentle acidity make them an excellent match for vegetables, cheeses, and herb-based dishes.

racha-lechkhumi.name: Racha-Lechkhumi
racha-lechkhumi.details: Racha-Lechkhumi is known for distinctive, often semi-sweet wines with a velvety taste. These wines have a soft structure, fruit-forward notes, and an elegant dessert-like character.

adjara.name: Adjara
adjara.details: Adjara, located near the Black Sea, produces fresh, light, and fruit-driven wines. The humid coastal climate gives them delicacy and a pleasant natural freshness.

samegrelo.name: Samegrelo
samegrelo.details: Samegrelo offers expressive, warm wines with a strong regional identity. Their flavour often combines fruity depth with lightly spicy notes and a long finish.

guria.name: Guria
guria.details: Guria is one of Georgia’s less obvious wine regions, valued for lively and original wines. They stand out for their freshness, lightly spicy character, and distinctive regional style.

1.description: A deep ruby wine with notes of dark fruit, spices, and gentle tannins. Fermented in traditional clay qvevri vessels.

2.description: A classic Saperavi aged in oak barrels. Intense aromas of blackberry and plum with a soft vanilla finish.

3.description: A natural wine made without added sulphur. Lively and fruit-driven, with bright acidity and freshness.

4.description: An elegant wine from the Mukuzani appellation. Velvety texture with notes of cherry, cedar, and delicate spices.

5.description: An iconic semi-sweet wine from the Alazani Valley. Rich aromas of raspberry and cherry with natural sweetness.

6.description: Soft and velvety, with aromas of ripe forest berries and a subtle sweetness on the finish.

7.description: A native variety from Kartli. Light and elegant, with notes of red fruit and herbs.

8.description: A delicate and floral wine made from the native Tavkveri grape. Notes of cherry, pomegranate, and rose petals.

9.description: A distinctive wine from the Racha region. Natural sweetness with aromas of raspberry and fig. Ideal with desserts.

10.description: A rare variety grown only in Racha. Silky texture with notes of blackberry and cocoa.

11.description: A legendary wine from Racha, often called the Georgian Pétrus. Intense aroma and a rich structure.

12.description: A light and fresh wine from coastal Guria. Delicate aromas of strawberries and flowers.

13.description: A traditional variety from Imeretia. Rustic and authentic, with earthy notes and dark fruit.

14.description: Intense and full-bodied. Oak barrels bring notes of vanilla and chocolate, followed by a long finish.

15.description: A Georgian interpretation of a classic variety. Blackcurrant, mint, and cedar with an elegant structure.

16.description: Certified organic wine. Fresh and fruit-driven, with a pure aroma and natural acidity.

17.description: Merlot fermented traditionally in qvevri. Soft tannins with aromas of plum and chocolate.

18.description: The winery’s flagship wine. Three years in barrel, elegant and complex, with ageing potential.

19.description: A monastery wine produced by monks for centuries. Authentic and soulful, with herbal notes.

20.description: A modern interpretation of a native variety. Fresh and elegant, with a floral aroma.

21.description: A clean and mineral white wine made from a native variety. Notes of green apple, citrus, and white flowers.

22.description: Aromatic and elegant. An intense bouquet of flowers, peach, and melon with refreshing acidity.

23.description: A native variety from Kartli. Light and fresh, with notes of pear, lime, and delicate minerality.

24.description: A typical Imeretian variety. Crisp and lively, with aromas of green fruit and a delicate bitter note.

25.description: The most popular white variety of Imeretia. Soft and rounded, with notes of peach and honey.

26.description: Full and rich, with notes of almond, vanilla, and ripe fruit. A long and creamy finish.

27.description: A rare variety from Kakheti. Golden and aromatic, with notes of apricot, honey, and warm spices.

28.description: A mountain variety from Kartli. Fresh and mineral, with a floral aroma and refreshing acidity.

29.description: Delicate sweetness with aromas of white flowers, peach, and apricot. Ideal with fish.

30.description: Oak-aged Rkatsiteli with notes of vanilla, butter, and ripe fruit. Full-bodied and elegant.

31.description: Fresh and aromatic, with notes of lemongrass, gooseberry, and lime. Perfect with salads.

32.description: Light and refreshing, with notes of green apple, citrus, and white flowers. A graceful aperitif.

33.description: A blend of two native Imeretian varieties. Complex and harmonious, with notes of fruit and minerals.

34.description: A rare monastery variety. Aromatic and complex, with notes of apricot, fig, and oriental spices.

35.description: Clean and elegant Mtsvane from Kakheti. Floral aroma with notes of citrus and white pepper.

36.description: An exceptionally rare variety from Guria. Delicate and floral, with a mineral finish.

37.description: Natural Tsolikouri made without sulphur. Lively and authentic, with distinctive fruit character.

38.description: Certified organic Rkatsiteli. Clean and mineral, with a long finish.

39.description: Aromatic Viognier in a Georgian style. An intense bouquet of peach, apricot, and violets.

40.description: Chardonnay fermented in qvevri. A unique meeting of a Western grape variety and Eastern tradition.

41.description: An iconic orange wine macerated on the skins for six months. Tannic and complex, with notes of tea and nuts.

42.description: A traditional Imeretian style. Several months of grape maceration create a rich and mineral wine.

43.description: A rare Kisi variety in amber style. Intense aromas of dried apricot, nuts, and spices.

44.description: A monastery amber wine made from a rare variety. Deep and meditative, with notes of fig and cardamom.

45.description: A modern interpretation of skin-contact wine. Fresh tannins and lively acidity with a fruit-driven aroma.

46.description: A traditional Imeretian wine macerated on the skins. Authentic and rustic, with a strong character.

47.description: A classic Imeretian style. Amber colour with notes of honey, nuts, and dried fruit.

48.description: Eight months of long maceration in qvevri. Tannic and powerful, with remarkable depth and complexity.

49.description: A mountain variety in amber style. Elegant tannins and a floral aroma with notes of dried fruit.

50.description: A full and rich amber wine from Imeretia. Intense aromas of almond, vanilla, and dried apricot.

51.description: A natural wine made without sulphur. Lively and dynamic, with notes of orange peel and tea.

52.description: Produced by the monks of Alaverdi Monastery. Traditional qvevri wine with a precious heritage.

53.description: The winery’s flagship amber wine. Long maceration and ageing create a monumental, collectible bottle.

54.description: Chinuri fermented in the Imeretian method. Moderate maceration with distinctive freshness.

55.description: An Imeretian blend in amber style. A harmonious combination of two varieties with rich tannic structure.

56.description: An exceptionally rare variety from Guria made in amber style. Unique and collectible.

57.description: A white mutation of Saperavi in amber style. Unusual and experimental, with impressive depth.

58.description: Twelve months of maceration in qvevri. Powerful and tannic, with remarkable aromatic complexity.

59.description: An elegant amber wine from a mountain variety. Delicate tannins and a floral aroma with nutty notes.

60.description: A blend of native Kakhetian varieties in amber style. Classic and representative of the region.

61.description: A fresh and fruit-driven rosé made from Saperavi. Aromas of strawberry, raspberry, and pomegranate with refreshing acidity.

62.description: Delicate and elegant, made from the native Tavkveri variety. Notes of rose petals and red fruit.

63.description: A light and floral rosé from coastal Guria. Aromas of strawberry, lime, and white flowers.

64.description: A rosé made from a native Kartli variety. Mineral and fresh, with notes of cherry and citrus.

65.description: The rustic character of Imeretia in a rosé expression. Authentic, with notes of wild berries.

66.description: An elegant rosé made from selected grapes. Intense fruit aroma and delicate minerality.

67.description: An unusual rosé from the rare Aladasturi variety. Delicate and floral, with a mineral finish.

68.description: Elegant Pinot Noir in a Provençal style. Pale pink, with aromas of strawberry and rose petals.

69.description: A lightly sparkling rosé made by the ancestral method. Lively and joyful, with bubbles and a fruit-driven aroma.

70.description: Traditional rosé fermented in qvevri. Unfiltered and authentic, with a mineral character.

71.description: A delicate and fruity Merlot rosé. Aromas of strawberry and cherry with subtle sweetness.

72.description: A sparkling rosé from Guria. Bubbles and a light aroma of strawberries and flowers. Ideal as an aperitif.

73.description: Certified organic rosé. Clean and fresh, with a natural aroma of red fruit.

74.description: A rosé blend of several native varieties. Complex and intriguing, with the character of Imeretia.

75.description: A natural rosé made without sulphur. Lively and dynamic, with light effervescence and a fruit-driven aroma.

76.description: A unique rosé with short skin maceration. Deeper in colour and more structured than a classic rosé.

77.description: A Provençal grape variety grown in Georgian terroir. Elegant and mineral, with aromas of dried flowers.

78.description: A prestigious rosé made from selected grapes. Complex and elegant, with a long finish.

79.description: A natural rosé from Imeretia. Rustic and authentic, with the character of native varieties.

80.description: A blend of Kakhetian varieties in a rosé expression. Fresh and fruit-driven, with mineral acidity.
